revisionRequest.description: Add TestimonialsCarousel instance with loopCarousel enabled to test page
revisionRequest.rationale: The test page (page ID 144) had no carousel instances with loop mode enabled. This is important because the inert attribute management on off-screen slides behaves differently with loop mode—Swiper duplicates slides in loop mode, which could affect the inert attribute logic.

blockAdded.position: After 'Manual Mode' demo, before 'Minimal' demo (4th demo container)
blockAdded.demoContainerDescription: Tests carousel with loop enabled for infinite scroll behavior and inert attribute handling with Swiper's duplicate slides.

blockAttributes.heading: Continuous Testimonials Loop
blockAttributes.description: This carousel loops infinitely, creating a seamless scrolling experience.

testableFeatures: Infinite loop behavior (seamless transition from last slide to first)
Inert attribute management with Swiper's duplicate slides
Navigation button behavior in loop mode
Pagination progress bar in loop mode

implementationNotes: Added a new demo-container block titled 'Loop Mode' with a descriptive explanation
Positioned after the 'Manual Mode' demo to group similar functional variations together
Used postsLimit: 6 to provide enough slides for testing loop behavior without overwhelming the test page
Enabled both showPagination and showNavigation as requested to make loop behavior fully testable
Used automatic post selection mode for consistency with most other demos
The JS code already has logic to handle loop mode: 'if ( ! ( element.dataset.loop === '1' ) )' on line 106 of TestimonialsCarousel.js
When loop is enabled, the inert attribute management is skipped (as designed) to avoid conflicts with Swiper's duplicate slides

codeReference.logic: The inert attribute management only runs when loop mode is DISABLED. This is intentional because Swiper creates duplicate slides in loop mode, and managing inert on duplicates would be complex. The comment on line 105 states: 'Manage inert attribute on inactive slides (when not in loop mode)'.

testingRecommendations.test: Navigate to the last slide using the next button, then click next again. The carousel should smoothly transition back to the first slide without jumping.
Inspect the DOM when loop is enabled. Swiper creates clone slides at the beginning and end of the slide list. Verify these clones do NOT have the inert attribute.
Tab through the carousel in loop mode. Focus should move through visible slides and skip any Swiper-created duplicate slides.
Watch the pagination progress bar as you navigate. In loop mode, it should reset when transitioning from last to first slide.

notes: No PHP/JS/TSX files were modified as requested—only WordPress page content was updated
The loop mode carousel is now the 4th demo on the test page, making it easy to locate
The existing JavaScript code already handles loop mode correctly by skipping inert attribute management when loop is enabled
This addition completes test coverage for both loop and non-loop carousel modes
